features.featureTitle: Update `frontend/src/hooks/useBroadcastMessages.ts` to use the ICP backend as the primary message store. On mount and on a polling interval (every 2 seconds), call `getMessages(since)` on the backend actor to fetch new messages and merge them into local React state. When sending a message, call `postMessage` on the backend actor to persist it, in addition to (or instead of) writing to localStorage. Continue broadcasting via BroadcastChannel for same-browser tab sync as a secondary optimization, but the backend must be the source of truth for cross-device delivery.

Ensure the `ChatMessage` TypeScript interface in `frontend/src/types/chat.ts` has an `id` field (string, required) that is unique per message (e.g., a UUID or timestamp+random string). All message creation points (user sends, bot responses, admin broadcasts, force text, big messages, system messages) must generate and assign a unique `id` before persisting. This id is used for deduplication when polling from the backend.
